headline: $4 A Month? Gene Munster Unpacks Trump's iPhone Tariff Math - Says 'Market Appears To Be Miscalculating the Impact'
summary: Gene Munster says Trump's iPhone tariff could raise prices modestly, but warns markets may be underestimating its broader economic impact.

headline: Trump's Apple Tariff Threat Hits Stock Hard, But Here's Why iPhones Can't Just Be 'Made in America'
summary: President Trump has demanded that Apple start manufacturing iPhones within the U.S. or be prepared to face a minimum 25% tariff on iPhones produced overseas.

headline: Morris Chang Saw Writing On The Wall 20 Years Ago For The Likes Of Intel And Samsung And Why They Would Never Be Able To Beat TSMC In Foundry Business
summary: In 2007, TSMC founder Morris Chang predicted that Intel and Samsung couldn't succeed in the foundry business due to fundamental cultural differences—and nearly two decades later, his foresight is proving accurate as TSMC maintains dominance while its rivals struggle to catch up.

headline: Benzinga Bulls And Bears: Alphabet, L3Harris, Target — And Latest Tariff News Roils Markets
summary: Benzinga examined the prospects for many investors' favorite stocks over the last week — here's a look at some of our top stories.

headline: Elon Musk Praises Google DeepMind's Veo 3 AI Video Model, Says 'It Is Awesome'
summary: Google DeepMind's Veo 3 is generating buzz for its ultra-realistic AI videos with synchronized audio, earning praise from Elon Musk.

headline: Trump Confirms 25% Tariff On Samsung And Other Companies Making Smartphones Outside The US
summary: Donald Trump confirms 25% tariff on iPhones will apply to Samsung and other non-US smartphone manufacturers.

headline: Prime Video Axes Rosamund Pike-Starrer 'The Wheel of Time:' Cost, Not Quality, Kills Fantasy Epic With 97% Rotten Tomatoes Score For The Latest Season
summary: Amazon's Prime Video has canceled The Wheel of Time after three seasons despite critical acclaim and a 97% Rotten Tomatoes score, citing high production costs and declining U.S. viewership as key reasons behind the decision.

headline: Oracle To Spend $40 Billion On Nvidia GB200 Chips For OpenAI's Texas Data Center: Report
summary: Oracle is reportedly spending $40 billion on 400,000 Nvidia AI chips to power OpenAI's massive new Texas data center.

headline: Apple Would Rather Absorb A 25% iPhone Tariff Than Move Production To The US, Says Analyst: '...No Guarantee They Won't Face Repeated Tariff Threats During His Term'
summary: Apple analyst Ming-Chi Kuo says the company is more likely to absorb a 25% tariff than move iPhone production to the US.

headline: Amazon's Zoox Issues Second Robotaxi Recall in a Month After San Francisco Crash
summary: Amazon's autonomous vehicle company Zoox has issued a second software recall for its Robotaxi after a collision in San Francisco.

headline: Elon Musk's Grok AI Is Quietly Being Deployed Across US Government Agencies — Experts Warn It Could Breach Privacy Laws And Create Serious Conflicts Of Interest: Report
summary: Elon Musk's AI chatbot Grok used in US gov agencies, sparking concerns over conflict of interest and privacy.

headline: Why Google's Demis Hassabis Disagrees With Co-Founder Sergey Brin On When AGI Will Arrive: 'I Have Quite A High Bar'
summary: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis says AGI is likely to arrive just after 2030 and argues it must meet a much higher standard than others—including Google co-founder Sergey Brin—believe, emphasizing true human-level thinking, creativity, and consistency.
